_comment: Curated remediation knowledge for grounding the fix-generation step. Each entry holds the CORRECT, verified fix (exact registry value / config directive / command) so retrieval steers the LLM away from hallucinated specifics (e.g. DisableSMBv1, allow_root). exploit_type/service tag what each addresses.

remediations.title: Remediate MS17-010 (EternalBlue) on Windows
remediations.text: Apply the Microsoft MS17-010 security update (e.g. KB4012212 / KB4012215 on Windows Server 2008 R2). If patching is not immediately possible, disable the SMBv1 server: set the registry value SMB1 (DWORD) to 0 under HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\LanmanServer\Parameters, e.g. reg add "HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\LanmanServer\Parameters" /v SMB1 /t REG_DWORD /d 0 /f, then REBOOT. The effective server-side control is SMB1=0 (there is no 'DisableSMBv1' value). Validate with nmap --script smb-vuln-ms17-010 -p445 <host>.

remediations.title: Disable anonymous FTP (vsftpd)
remediations.text: If the FTP server accepts anonymous logins (the 'ftp' account accepting any password), set anonymous_enable=NO in /etc/vsftpd.conf and ensure local_enable=YES, then restart so the change takes effect (service vsftpd restart; if launched from (x)inetd, ensure the daemon is invoked with the config file or run it standalone with listen=YES). There is no 'allow_root' directive for this.

remediations.title: Eliminate weak/default credentials (FTP and SSH)
remediations.text: Change all default/weak account passwords (e.g. msfadmin, service) to strong, unique values and disable or remove unused default accounts. Enforce a password policy. These accounts are shared by FTP and SSH on Unix hosts, so rotating them remediates both credential brute-force vectors.

remediations.title: Harden SSH against credential brute force
remediations.text: Rotate weak/default account passwords to strong values. Prefer key-based authentication and set 'PasswordAuthentication no' in /etc/ssh/sshd_config, set 'PermitRootLogin no', then reload sshd (service ssh reload). Deploy fail2ban to throttle repeated failed logins.

remediations.title: Patch Shellshock (CVE-2014-6271 / CVE-2014-7169)
remediations.text: Update bash to a patched build: apt-get update && apt-get install --only-upgrade bash (or the distro equivalent). Verify the fix: env x='() { :;}; echo vulnerable' bash -c true should NOT print 'vulnerable'. Disable unneeded CGI (a2dismod cgi cgid) and restart Apache (service apache2 restart).

remediations.title: Remediate Samba usermap_script RCE (CVE-2007-2447)
remediations.text: Upgrade Samba to a fixed release (>= 3.0.25). As mitigation, remove any 'username map script' directive from smb.conf and restart smbd. (Distinct from MS17-010, which is a Windows SMBv1 flaw.)
